title: OpenClaw in Your Hand: Building a Physical AI Terminal - Lech Kalinowski, Callstack
summary: Vault is a text-first handheld AI terminal built on ESP32-S3 with dual displays (OLED and e-paper) that runs a local 120B parameter model via NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM. The device operates in four modes and enables autonomous OpenClaw agent execution with real-time tool calling visualization on e-paper.
writeup: This talk covers Vault, a text-first handheld AI terminal built on the ESP32-S3 microcontroller with dual displays (an OLED and an e-paper screen) that runs local language models through TensorRT-LLM and supports autonomous agent execution. The architecture is modular: firmware lives on the microcontroller while a Python backend handles model inference, agents, and game logic. The build illustrates how local inference, self-hosting, and agent execution can be packaged into dedicated hardware rather than a phone or laptop, with inference optimization carrying the load on constrained devices.
